name: Mushroom Tofu Verde
recipeIngredient: 12 oz brown mushrooms, halved or quartered depending on size
1 16-ounce container of fresh green salsa (see note)
6 oz extra firm, low-fat tofu, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
Cilantro, green onion, crushed red chili for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. In a pan, sauté the diced onion until translucent.
2. Add minced garlic and red bell pepper to the pan. Cook until fragrant and vegetables are softened.
3. Sprinkle cumin seeds over the mixture and stir to combine.
4. Add the brown mushrooms and cook until they begin to release their juices.
5. Pour in the fresh green salsa (ensure you have enough for the tofu and mushroom mixture).
6. Add the cubed tofu and stir to coat evenly with the sauce.
7. If using, add vegetable stock powder and mix well.
8. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes until flavors are well combined and slightly thickened.
9. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
10. Serve over rice (such as Spanish rice) topped with cilantro, green onion, and crushed red chili.
Note: Fresh green salsa can be made using tomatillo, mild green chilies, jalapeño, onion, garlic, cilantro, and lime. Alternatively, you can use a jar of fresh salsa or Mrs. Renfrow's brand.
